steps.title: Recognize the standard form of a circle
steps.content: A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ has the equation $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$. We can read the center directly from this form without any algebra.

steps.title: Extract the center from the given equation
steps.content:
The given equation is $(x-1)^2 + (y+7)^2 = 1$. Comparing to the standard form:
  • $(x-1)^2$ corresponds to $(x-h)^2$, so $h = 1$
  • $(y+7)^2 = (y-(-7))^2$ corresponds to $(y-k)^2$, so $k = -7$
Therefore, the center is $(1, -7)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
